text: Chapter 13 ("Snowfall") contains no load-bearing prose passages — composed entirely of notated music staves, chord symbols, Roman-numeral position markers, fingerings, and performance directions (freely, a Tempo, let ring, rit., D.S. al Coda). The OCR transcript yields only musical-notation artifacts (measure numbers, chord labels, position markers like II/IV/VII) rather than extractable teaching prose. Bertoncini's characteristic moves — the 'little orchestra' textural conception, open-string voicings, counterpoint, cluster voicings, parenthesized recovered notes from the recording — are visible only in the notated arrangement itself, not in prose annotations within this chapter.
